safety_boundary: Keep as a compatibility/reference pattern; do not execute command workflows or third-party writes without explicit approval and ECC validation.
what_to_copy: Command directory layout, concise command naming, and mapping pattern from user-facing entry points to skills.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy command-first architecture as the primary ECC surface; avoid importing external publishing or connector assumptions.

real_life_job: Present a reusable content-production plugin package with skills, commands, quality gates, manifests, setup instructions, and cross-surface compatibility notes.
input_contract: README catalog, supported surface table, skills list, command list, brand setup inputs, plugin manifests, scripts, and connector notes.
output_artifact: Reusable package-shape reference, skill catalog classification, router match, or expansion plan.
validation_gate: Check canonical branch, README freshness, manifest paths, skill count consistency, and whether claims are backed by repo files before copying package structure.
human_review_boundary: Optional for package-shape cataloging; required before generating client-facing content, publishing to CMS, using connectors, or relying on compliance-related outputs.
safety_boundary: Use as reference only; copied patterns need a separate ECC contract, explicit source attribution, connector opt-in, and review boundaries for generated/published content.
what_to_copy: README package anatomy, supported-surface matrix, skill catalog table, command-to-skill relationship, manifest path documentation, and validation/release consistency checks.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy domain-specific marketing pipeline behavior, model/version claims, publishing workflows, or compliance assertions without current verification and target-workflow need.

real_life_job: Study an executor-pipeline surface as inspiration for how work units are invoked, bounded, and validated, then translate only relevant concepts into Codex/ECC catalog language.
input_contract: Repo docs, source links, category indexes, and user-provided context.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan.
validation_gate: Verify the executor path or repository index before making any stronger claim; map any execution concept to explicit Codex/ECC inputs, outputs, and safety gates.
human_review_boundary: Recommended before sending, publishing, or writing externally.
safety_boundary: Do not treat the runtime platform as Codex architecture unless explicitly chosen.
what_to_copy: Confirmed patterns for executor boundaries, task packaging, and pipeline-stage naming.
what_to_ignore: Runtime-specific worker model, service assumptions, external dependencies, and non-Codex execution architecture.

real_life_job: Classify a flows guide as runtime workflow inspiration for reusable workflow patterns, skill or package ideas, and router matching.
input_contract: Repo docs, skill files, manifests, and category indexes.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern, reusable skill or package idea, and router match.
validation_gate: Read the flows guide or README before citing behavior; keep any translation grounded in explicit workflow contracts, inputs, outputs, and review gates.
human_review_boundary: Recommended before sending, publishing, or writing externally.
safety_boundary: Do not treat the runtime platform as Codex architecture unless explicitly chosen.
what_to_copy: Confirmed flow concepts that help describe reusable workflow stages, router matches, or package boundaries.
what_to_ignore: Runtime-specific flow APIs, deployment expectations, and any behavior not directly evidenced by the guide.

real_life_job: Catalog lightweight reusable skills for local/offline tasks such as code explanation, commit messages, meeting notes, pricing advice, SQL explanation, summaries, and tone shifts.
input_contract: Skill folder list, README install URL, prompts, SKILL.md metadata, scripts/index.html, and optional assets/webview.html.
output_artifact: Reusable skill/package pattern showing a skill folder with instructions, executable script entry point, and optional UI asset.
validation_gate: Confirm folder list and required files; inspect a specific skill folder before treating the script or UI shape as reusable.
human_review_boundary: Optional for catalog lookup; required before executing or distributing any script-backed skill.
safety_boundary: Reference only; script-backed skills require code review and sandboxing before use in an ECC workflow.
what_to_copy: Folder shape with SKILL.md plus script entry point, install URL pattern, sample prompts, and optional UI asset convention.
what_to_ignore: App-specific installation steps, device-specific assumptions, and any executable behavior not inspected at the individual skill level.

real_life_job: Catalog small reusable skills for developer workflow tasks such as commit writing, debugging checklists, cost comparison, architecture sketching, and cost audits.
input_contract: Skill folder, README table, skill names, install rows, and SKILL.md format guidance with YAML frontmatter.
output_artifact: Reusable config-package pattern for a skill registry row and router match.
validation_gate: Confirm folder list and README format reference; inspect individual SKILL.md files before copying any behavior into a new ECC skill.
human_review_boundary: Optional for catalog lookup; required before adopting any generated commit, architecture, or audit output that affects a real repo or person.
safety_boundary: Reference only until translated into an ECC contract; do not copy external instructions wholesale without review for tool permissions and write boundaries.
what_to_copy: Skill folder table, concise SKILL.md frontmatter convention, trigger-heavy descriptions, and short skill-size guidance.
what_to_ignore: Runtime-specific invocation assumptions, slash-command wording, and any framework-specific install path not needed for Codex/ECC.

real_life_job: Maintain a division manifest that maps top-level source directories to catalog labels, icons, and colors for browsing and validation.
input_contract: Top-level division directories, divisions.json entries, display metadata, and validation scripts that compare the manifest to directories and converter/linter config.
output_artifact: Config manifest for category routing, catalog display, and consistency checks.
validation_gate: Run or mirror a consistency check that compares manifest entries with source directories and conversion/lint configuration before adopting the taxonomy.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless category changes affect a person, external system, or published catalog.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; manifest categories do not imply operational behavior by themselves.
what_to_copy: Source-of-truth manifest concept, display metadata fields, add-a-division checklist, and consistency-check gate.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy the exact division taxonomy unless it matches the local catalog contract.

real_life_job: Browse a specialized category folder to identify candidate reusable agent/workflow surfaces for catalog routing.
input_contract: Canonical specialized folder listing, source files, repo index, and division manifest entry for the specialized category.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan for selected specialized entries.
validation_gate: Open the specific source file for any selected specialized entry before describing capabilities or copying patterns.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless output affects a person or external system.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; folder membership alone is not enough to infer workflow behavior.
what_to_copy: Category folder as router surface, one-file-per-entry organization, and canonicalized path handling for stale catalog links.
what_to_ignore: Do not infer specific workflow behavior from filenames alone.

real_life_job: Browse a strategy folder containing coordination, playbook, runbook, quickstart, and executive brief surfaces for reusable workflow lookup.
input_contract: Strategy directory listing, nested coordination/playbook/runbook folders, and visible markdown strategy documents.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan for strategy or playbook-style catalog entries.
validation_gate: Open the relevant playbook or runbook file before extracting any process steps or adopting a workflow pattern.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless output affects a person or external system.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; do not execute playbook actions from the folder listing alone.
what_to_copy: Folder-level separation of strategy surfaces and the idea of routing quickstarts, runbooks, and playbooks separately.
what_to_ignore: Do not invent playbook content, decision rules, or operating procedures from the directory listing alone.

real_life_job: Maintain a tools manifest that defines supported tool targets, install contracts, rendering formats, destination templates, detection paths, and validation requirements.
input_contract: tools.json entries, converter/install scripts, required fields, supported format names, install kind, scope, destination templates, and detection/version metadata.
output_artifact: Config manifest for tool indexing, install/render routing, and consistency validation.
validation_gate: Validate required fields and compare manifest entries with converter/install script support before adopting any tool target.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless output affects a person, external system, or user environment installation.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; require explicit approval before installing tools or modifying user/project config.
what_to_copy: Manifest-as-source-of-truth pattern, required-field validation, explicit installKind, render format contract, scope metadata, and destination templates.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy unsupported tool targets, install commands, or user-environment modification behavior without a local approval boundary.

real_life_job: Use a skill/package bundle as a reference when shaping reusable Codex/ECC agent workflow configuration.
input_contract: Repo docs, skill files, manifests, category indexes, and package metadata for the referenced bundle.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern summary, reusable skill/package idea, or router match for later ECC contract work.
validation_gate: Confirm source reachability and inspect manifest or skill files before copying any pattern into a repo workflow.
human_review_boundary: Optional for reference-only cataloging; required before output affects a person, external system, or project workflow contract.
safety_boundary: Keep as a reference pattern until deliberately copied into an ECC contract with explicit action boundaries.
what_to_copy: High-level package organization, manifest fields, registry conventions, and routing metadata if verified.
what_to_ignore: Unverified operational claims, external action behavior, and any package-specific assumptions not visible in source.

real_life_job: Reference a packaged web-app-builder bundle shape for reusable Codex/ECC workflow cataloging.
input_contract: Repository folder and plugin package files; exact page shows package folder containing .codex-plugin, skills, and compatibility metadata.
output_artifact: Catalog row describing a config-package surface and possible router expansion target.
validation_gate: Confirm package folder is reachable and contains expected package surfaces before copying any pattern into an ECC contract.
human_review_boundary: Optional for cataloging; required before using the package shape to affect people, production systems, or third-party resources.
safety_boundary: Reference only until translated into a local ECC workflow contract with explicit input, output, validation, and write boundaries.
what_to_copy: Package layout idea: plugin metadata plus nested skills as a reusable bundle surface.
what_to_ignore: Do not infer implementation behavior from the sparse folder listing alone.

real_life_job: Look up available bundles from an index so a reusable Codex/ECC catalog can route users to the right package reference.
input_contract: Bundle index documentation, source links, category indexes, and user-provided lookup context.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan for a bundle or bundle category.
validation_gate: Verify the bundle index is reachable and current before using it as authoritative routing data.
human_review_boundary: Optional for catalog lookup; required before selected bundle guidance changes a project or external system.
safety_boundary: Treat as a registry reference only until a selected bundle is inspected and translated into an ECC contract.
what_to_copy: Index structure, category naming, bundle metadata shape, and source-link conventions if verified.
what_to_ignore: Assumed bundle capabilities not supported by the index or linked package contents.

real_life_job: Guide a team through deciding whether DDD fits, modeling domain boundaries, and applying tactical/evented patterns where justified.
input_contract: Current system context, domain expert or product-owner proxy, business goals, integration landscape, and agreement on key domain outcomes.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern with steps for DDD fit assessment, strategic model, context mapping, tactical model, and selective evented design.
validation_gate: Evidence should preserve the workflow steps and avoid treating DDD as automatic; first gate is deciding whether full, partial, or lightweight modeling is justified.
human_review_boundary: Domain expert or product-owner review is needed before freezing model boundaries or implementation decisions.
safety_boundary: Keep as a reference workflow until adapted into an ECC contract; do not mutate architecture or code from catalog classification alone.
what_to_copy: The staged progression: assess fit, create strategic model, map contexts, implement tactical model, adopt evented patterns selectively.
what_to_ignore: Do not assume event sourcing, CQRS, or sagas are required; the source frames them as selective.

real_life_job: Use a machine-readable bundle registry as structured input for reusable Codex/ECC catalog lookup and routing.
input_contract: Machine-readable bundle JSON, repo docs, source links, category indexes, and user-provided context.
output_artifact: Matched router surface, structured bundle metadata, and expansion plan for downstream inspection.
validation_gate: Fetch and validate JSON syntax/schema before treating entries as authoritative catalog data.
human_review_boundary: Optional for internal catalog matching; required before generated routing affects users, project workflows, or external systems.
safety_boundary: Keep as structured reference data until selected entries are verified and translated into explicit ECC contracts.
what_to_copy: Verified JSON field schema, identifiers, category mappings, and source-link structure.
what_to_ignore: Any inferred behavior beyond the structured metadata and any stale entries not confirmed against source files.

real_life_job: Browse a marketplace manifest to identify installable local plugin bundles, categories, source paths, and installation policy shape for a reusable Codex/ECC catalog.
input_contract: Repository manifest JSON with plugin entries, local source paths, category labels, and policy fields.
output_artifact: Matched config-package surface with registry fields, category mapping, and expansion plan for possible ECC contract reuse.
validation_gate: Confirm the manifest page is reachable, parseable as JSON, and contains plugin entries with source.path, policy.installation, policy.authentication, and category fields.
human_review_boundary: Optional for cataloging; required before copying package entries into an operational ECC contract or installing any bundle.
safety_boundary: Reference-only classification; do not install, execute, or copy into active workflow config without explicit review.
what_to_copy: Manifest field structure: package name, display metadata, plugins array, local source path, install availability, authentication policy, category grouping.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy package contents, product-specific naming, or installation assumptions without an ECC contract and verification.

real_life_job: Look up skill records by id, path, category, description, risk, source, setup requirements, and platform support for a reusable workflow catalog.
input_contract: Large JSON skills index containing skill metadata, paths, categories, descriptions, risk labels, source labels, dates, platform targets, and setup summaries.
output_artifact: Normalized skill registry surface with routing metadata and candidate expansion paths.
validation_gate: Confirm the exact index is reachable and representative records expose path, category, description, risk, source, and platform/setup metadata.
human_review_boundary: Optional for lookup; required if a matched skill will drive user-facing, external, regulated, or irreversible actions.
safety_boundary: Treat as metadata reference only; do not assume listed skills are safe, current, or locally installed without separate verification.
what_to_copy: Index record schema concepts: id, path, category, description, risk, source, setup, platform support, and date metadata.
what_to_ignore: Do not import skill behavior, risk labels, or platform support claims as trusted without reading the underlying skill files.

real_life_job: Help a user choose and execute a workflow playbook that combines multiple skills toward one concrete outcome.
input_contract: Immediate user goal, matching workflow, prerequisites, related bundles, and step-specific skill references.
output_artifact: Workflow index entry or matched playbook with ordered steps, prompt examples, and linked skills.
validation_gate: Check that the selected workflow has prerequisites, ordered steps, skill links, and expected artifacts before treating it as reusable.
human_review_boundary: Optional for choosing a workflow; review required before outputs affect external systems, architecture, security testing, or release actions.
safety_boundary: Use as a catalog/index reference only; do not execute linked workflows without the target repo contract and permissions.
what_to_copy: Index pattern: workflow definition, prerequisites, ordered steps, linked skills, prompt examples, and machine-readable metadata pointer.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy unrelated workflows into a specific ECC lane without a repeated job and input/output contract.

real_life_job: Browse plugin bundle folders to understand how reusable packages are grouped as installable directory surfaces.
input_contract: Repository folder listing of plugin bundle directories plus related manifest references.
output_artifact: Catalog door classification for plugin bundle directory organization and package expansion paths.
validation_gate: Confirm the folder is reachable and contains multiple package directories that correspond to manifest source paths.
human_review_boundary: Optional for reference cataloging; required before installing, vendoring, or adapting a bundle.
safety_boundary: Reference-only; do not execute bundle scripts or copy directories into an ECC workflow without review.
what_to_copy: Directory-level package grouping, bundle naming, and manifest-to-folder linkage pattern.
what_to_ignore: Do not infer full workflow behavior from folder names alone; inspect nested files before reuse.

real_life_job: Browse a skill library folder to identify reusable skill package conventions and category coverage for workflow routing.
input_contract: Skills directory listing plus README guidance describing per-skill folders, required SKILL.md files, optional scripts, examples, and resources.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern reference for reusable skill/package ideas and router matches.
validation_gate: Confirm the folder and README are reachable, and verify representative skills before making behavior-level claims.
human_review_boundary: Optional for structural lookup; required before using any skill output to affect a person or external system.
safety_boundary: Reference-only until a specific skill is read and copied into an ECC contract with explicit safety gates.
what_to_copy: Skill folder convention: one folder per skill, required SKILL.md, optional scripts, examples, and resources, plus category grouping.
what_to_ignore: Do not treat directory presence as proof of quality, safety, or local availability; avoid copying broad library assumptions.

real_life_job: Route build-related workflow lookup requests to a reusable skill/package reference for build configuration patterns.
input_contract: Repo docs, skill files, manifests, category indexes, or other inspectable package metadata for the /build door.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern, reusable skill/package idea, or router match for build workflow use cases.
validation_gate: Confirm the source page, README, or raw skill files before copying behavior into an ECC contract.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless output affects a person or external system.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract.
what_to_copy: Door naming, category placement, config-package framing, and manifest/registry shape if verified.
what_to_ignore: Any build automation behavior not visible in verified source files.

real_life_job: Route deployment or operations workflow lookup requests to a reusable skill/package reference for DevOps configuration patterns.
input_contract: Repo docs, skill files, manifests, category indexes, or other inspectable package metadata for the /devops door.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern, reusable skill/package idea, or router match for DevOps workflow use cases.
validation_gate: Confirm the source page, README, or raw skill files before copying behavior into an ECC contract.

safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract.
what_to_copy: Door naming, deploy category placement, config-package framing, and manifest/registry shape if verified.
what_to_ignore: Any deployment execution behavior not visible in verified source files.

real_life_job: Route integration workflow lookup requests to a reusable skill/package reference for integration configuration patterns.
input_contract: Repo docs, skill files, manifests, category indexes, or other inspectable package metadata for the /integrate door.
output_artifact: Workflow pattern, reusable skill/package idea, or router match for integration workflow use cases.
validation_gate: Confirm the source page, README, or raw skill files before copying behavior into an ECC contract.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless output affects a person or external system.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract.
what_to_copy: Door naming, integration category placement, config-package framing, and manifest/registry shape if verified.
what_to_ignore: Any integration behavior not visible in verified source files.

real_life_job: Browse a structured registry of reusable skills by category, skill path, description, format rules, and design patterns.
input_contract: Repository README, category folders, skill names, SKILL.md files, category index, and marketplace-style metadata fields.
output_artifact: Catalog row, router match, reusable workflow-package pattern, or expansion plan grounded in the registry structure.
validation_gate: Check that categories, paths, and metadata fields match the README; treat README descriptions as catalog evidence, not proof of runtime behavior.
human_review_boundary: Optional unless the selected registry idea will be copied into a live workflow, affect users, or trigger external actions.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract with explicit input, output, validation, and action boundaries.
what_to_copy: Copy the category taxonomy, path convention, metadata schema, quality scoring dimensions, and orchestrator-versus-standalone distinction.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy broad marketplace claims or runtime behavior without checking the specific SKILL.md file.

real_life_job: Use a build-category registry surface to identify project scaffolding and build-pipeline skills, including the standalone build workflow.
input_contract: Repository README, build category folder, source links, category indexes, and user-provided build context such as idea, spec, or comparable product.
output_artifact: Matched build router surface, reusable build-pipeline idea, or expansion plan for a local ECC workflow contract.
validation_gate: Confirm whether the request refers to the build category or the nested build skill before copying behavior; check the specific SKILL.md for actual phases.
human_review_boundary: Human review is needed before generating or changing a real project; catalog matching alone can remain optional-review.
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; do not execute build actions, install dependencies, or modify files during cataloging.
what_to_copy: Copy the category-folder-to-specific-skill disambiguation, project-scaffolding category framing, and standalone-skill routing idea.
what_to_ignore: Do not assume all build-category skills share the nested build skill behavior; avoid copying implementation phases without a dedicated source read.

real_life_job: Analyze an app from video, screenshots, URL, or description, then convert product analysis into ordered engineering implementation stories.
input_contract: Video file, screen recording, screenshots, URL, app description, or a combination of those inputs.
output_artifact: Product analysis plus generated engineering stories ordered by implementation dependency, with BE/FE counts and next-step recommendations.
validation_gate: Every story must trace back to phase 1 feature or story candidate; self-healing validation up to 3 iterations with tests/build where applicable.
human_review_boundary: optional unless output affects a person or external system
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; specs should remain draft planning artifacts until reviewed for scope, feasibility, and safety.
what_to_copy: Input modes, phase handoff from analysis to spec, traceability requirement, implementation ordering, concise output summary.
what_to_ignore: No-question autonomy and assumptions about downstream story commands unless adapted to local Codex/ECC surfaces.

real_life_job: Look up a reusable QA-related skill or package reference from a registry-like catalog.
input_contract: Repo docs, source links, category indexes, or user-provided catalog context.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan for a reusable Codex/ECC lookup catalog.
validation_gate: Before reuse, verify the GitHub category folder or repository README and confirm any manifest or package shape directly from source.
human_review_boundary: Optional for catalog lookup; required if resulting output affects a person, external system, or operational QA decision.
safety_boundary: Keep as a reference pattern until copied into an explicit ECC contract with source attribution and review gates.
what_to_copy: Only the high-level registry/package shape after direct source verification.
what_to_ignore: Do not infer QA workflow steps, tests, agents, or operational behavior from the sparse catalog hint alone.

real_life_job: Reference a reusable shipping or release-pipeline skill package shape when matching future Codex/ECC workflow catalog entries.
input_contract: Repository docs, combo directory path, package or skill manifests if present, and category indexes.
output_artifact: Matched router surface or reusable skill/package reference note; not a newly designed workflow.
validation_gate: Confirm the canonical repository path and package contents before copying any pattern into an ECC contract.
human_review_boundary: Optional for catalog lookup; required before applying the pattern to a workflow that affects a person, deployment, or external system.
safety_boundary: Reference-only until translated into a scoped ECC contract with explicit inputs, outputs, validation, and action boundaries.
what_to_copy: Only the high-level package/registry shape after live source verification.
what_to_ignore: Do not infer release behavior, deployment authority, or operational automation from the door name alone.

real_life_job: Reference a test-oriented registry surface when matching future reusable workflow package or router entries.
input_contract: Repository docs, test directory contents if reachable, source links, category indexes, and user-provided context.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan grounded in verified repository contents when available.
validation_gate: Verify whether the test directory contains runnable tests, fixtures, manifests, or placeholder content before assigning stronger meaning.
human_review_boundary: Optional for catalog lookup; required before applying any test package pattern to external or person-affecting automation.
safety_boundary: Reference-only until copied into an ECC contract with scoped validation behavior.
what_to_copy: Only verified directory structure, manifest conventions, or fixture patterns after source confirmation.
what_to_ignore: Do not treat the generic name as proof of a testing workflow, CI workflow, or quality gate.

real_life_job: Define the required structure and metadata for portable skill packages before adding them to a registry or adapting them for Codex/ECC.
input_contract: Skill spec document, frontmatter schema, directory structure rules, field definitions, and validation expectations.
output_artifact: Reusable skill/package specification reference for router matching, manifest design, and ECC skill-shape comparison.
validation_gate: Check the file is reachable, preserve required schema semantics, and validate any adapted local skill against the target ECC/Codex contract.
human_review_boundary: Optional for cataloging; required before publishing a skill, changing a shared registry, or claiming compatibility with a runtime.
safety_boundary: Use as a reference format only; operational safety still belongs in the copied ECC skill contract and validation gates.
what_to_copy: Directory shape, required frontmatter concepts, slug discipline, source attribution field, and verification metadata concept.
what_to_ignore: Do not inherit framework-specific installer assumptions, deprecated aliases, or public-registry claims unless they match the local Codex/ECC surface.

real_life_job: Browse lightweight single-agent example ideas and task categories as inspiration for reusable Codex/ECC workflow surfaces.
input_contract: Repository README framework/use-case section, linked example entries, directory hints from the supplied catalog, and user-provided source URL.
output_artifact: Inspiration-catalog classification with a note that the exact directory link was unavailable and evidence came from the repository README.
validation_gate: Before reuse, open the specific linked example or canonical path that exists, verify runnable inputs/outputs, and confirm it maps to an ECC contract rather than treating README rows as full evidence.
human_review_boundary: Optional for cataloging; required before running examples with external APIs, user data, legal/financial outputs, or third-party writes.
safety_boundary: Use only as inspiration until a real source file is inspected and copied into an explicit ECC contract with validation and review gates.
what_to_copy: Example categories, lightweight single-agent task framing, and candidate router labels after source-file verification.
what_to_ignore: Do not copy code, dependency assumptions, or claimed behavior from the broken path; do not treat README examples as validated workflows.

real_life_job: Diagnose an incident or urgent bug, apply a minimal hotfix, verify preflight safety, deploy when configured, and confirm service health.
input_contract: Bug description, error message, stack trace, affected area, or broken behavior supplied as arguments.
output_artifact: Fix and Ship report with incident timeline, severity, root cause, fix summary, preflight verdict, deployment target/status, health check, MTTR, and final status.
validation_gate: Hotfix must succeed, preflight must be ready, deployment must not fail, health checks/log checks must pass; rollback strategy activates on failure.
human_review_boundary: optional unless output affects a person or external system
safety_boundary: Keep as reference pattern until copied into an ECC contract; require explicit approval for real deployment, rollback, pushing, merging, or third-party writes.
what_to_copy: Incident timeline, severity classification, deploy target detection order, health verification checklist, rollback branches, final report shape.
what_to_ignore: Autonomous deployment, issue comments, closing issues, pushing, merging, or production actions without explicit local approval.

real_life_job: Route a user's repo or source context to the most relevant runtime-style query surface for inspiration, then translate any useful pattern back into Codex/ECC terms.
input_contract: Repo docs, source links, category indexes, and user-provided context.
output_artifact: Matched router surface and expansion plan.
validation_gate: Confirm the source path and README before using this as more than inspiration; verify that any copied pattern has a Codex/ECC equivalent.
human_review_boundary: Recommended before sending, publishing, or writing externally.
safety_boundary: Do not treat the runtime platform as Codex architecture unless explicitly chosen.
what_to_copy: High-level separation of core query routing concepts if confirmed in source.
what_to_ignore: Implementation-specific runtime assumptions, framework lifecycle, and any platform architecture that does not map cleanly to Codex/ECC.
